description:
The Devil's AI Dictionary cuts through AI hype with sharp, plain-English definitions of AI terms, product labels, and buzzwords. Every entry pairs a devil's definition with a plain definition, then explains why the term exists, how it gets misused, and what it means in practice.

Browse A-Z, move by category, or search across 700+ entries covering machine learning, large language models, agents, automation, embeddings, infrastructure, vendors, economics, safety, and the rest of the AI lexicon. Save your place, jump in from deep links, and choose from four reading themes: Book, Codex, Absolutely, and Night.

No accounts. No tracking. No ads. Just a sceptical dictionary for people who want AI jargon explained clearly instead of performed at.
whatsNew:
Version 1.1.0 turns the reference web into a real one.

See Also and vendor references now resolve into proper dictionary links across web, Apple, and Android, while unresolved labels have become full entries instead of loose ends. The catalogue now covers hundreds more terms across agents, workflows, infrastructure, safety, economics, vendors, and the weird culture around AI.

This release also hardens the Cloudflare catalogue path so the larger dictionary stays fast, keeps the mobile catalogue backward-compatible, and prepares the app for clearer update messaging if a future catalogue ever needs a newer build.
